title: lepidagathis sabui
intro: Lepidagathis sabui is a rare and endangered plant species found in the Eastern Himalayas, belonging to the family Acanthaceae and commonly known as the "sabui plant." Traditionally, Lepidagathis sabui has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been reported to possess several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, Lepidagathis sabui is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions.
